score8.problem: Order errors are rising (3%), and negative reviews are scaling with volume.
score8.constraint: We can only lead one major change this quarter: fix errors or launch the campaign.
score8.options: Fix errors now (misses revenue goals) or launch the campaign (scales errors to 300/week).
score8.recommendation: I recommend we delay the campaign to build the automation. Sustainable growth is impossible with a 3% error rate.
score8.metric: Error rate returns to 1.5% baseline within 6 weeks.

score10.problem: The business is prioritizing immediate revenue, but launching now will scale negative brand sentiment beyond the point of recovery.
score10.constraint: One-initiative limit; political pressure for revenue vs. technical need for fulfillment stability.
score10.options: Launch on time to hit targets (accepting ~300 errors/week) or delay to protect the brand (accepting a revenue miss).
score10.recommendation: I am explicitly choosing brand health over quarterly revenue. We will delay the launch until the fulfillment automation is live.
score10.metric: Refund costs return to 1.5% baseline; revenue recovery target set for next quarter.
score10.backfire: If the automation fails to move these metrics or the revenue miss exceeds the long-term benefit, I accept that leadership will question my decision-making authority.

score9.problem: Turnaround time is up to 9 days. Automation is a quick fix, but Sales data quality is the root cause.
score9.constraint: Limited to one major change this quarter.
score9.options: Automate routing (hides bad data) or fix Sales intake (slows revenue but solves the root cause).
score9.recommendation: We must push back on Sales to fix intake quality. I am prioritizing clean data over a fast automation fix.
score9.metric: Sales submission error rate drops by 50% in 30 days.
score9.risk: I accept the risk that slowing down submissions will cause immediate friction with Sales during a strong revenue quarter.

score10.problem: The current metric rewards volume, causing the team to ignore complex cases and inflating the turnaround time.
score10.constraint: One structural change limit; executives want visible output progress.
score10.options: Build a dashboard (visibility without fix) or change metrics (fixes behavior but visible output will drop).
score10.recommendation: We will change the performance metric to reward case age. I am not building the dashboard this quarter.
score10.metric: Average age of cases in queue drops from 10 days to 5 days by year-end.
score10.backfire: If changing the metric drops volume without actually improving turnaround time, I accept that leadership will view my decision as an operational failure.
